title: What's Stopping Me?
description: Identify your personal barriers and receive specific strategies to overcome them.

questions.question: When you stop studying English for a few days, the main reason is usually:
options: "I switched to a new app or method" (shiny object)
"I feel embarrassed when I make mistakes" (perfectionism)
questions.explanation: The plateau effect is the #1 motivation killer. Solution: measure progress with concrete tests, not feelings.

questions.question: The most effective way to overcome the plateau effect is:
options: Take a level test to see concrete, measurable progress
Start the whole course from zero
questions.explanation: Concrete measurement shows real progress that feelings miss. You ARE improving, even when it doesn't feel like it.

questions.question: According to the science of habits, the best way to build a daily English practice is:
options: Study only when you feel motivated
Attach it to an existing habit and start ridiculously small
Wait for the perfect moment to begin
questions.explanation: Habit stacking + tiny start: "After my morning coffee, I do 1 flashcard." Then it grows naturally.

questions.question: A growth mindset means believing that abilities can be ___ through effort and practice.
questions.explanation: Growth mindset = abilities are developed through effort, not fixed at birth. The key belief for learning.

questions.question: "I can't speak English fluently." How do you transform this with a growth mindset?
questions.explanation: The word "YET" transforms a limitation into a process. You're on a journey, not at a dead end.

questions.question: You've been using Duolingo for 2 weeks and see a new app advertised. You should:
options: Delete Duolingo and try the new one
Commit to Duolingo for at least 90 days before evaluating
questions.explanation: Shiny object syndrome kills progress. Commit to ONE system for 90 days before judging it.

questions.question: The "Don't Break the ___" method means maintaining a daily streak of practice.
questions.explanation: Jerry Seinfeld's productivity method: mark each practice day on a calendar. Don't break the chain.

questions.question: "I don't have time to study English." The best counter-strategy is:
options: Wait until you have more free time
Do 10-minute micro-sessions attached to existing habits
Study only on weekends for 4 hours
questions.explanation: 10 minutes daily > 0 minutes daily. Attach practice to something you already do (commute, coffee, lunch).

questions.question: What is "delayed gratification" in language learning?
options: Resisting the urge to quit now for greater results later
questions.explanation: Language learning is a long-term investment. The ability to keep going despite slow visible progress = delayed gratification.

questions.question: The MOST powerful thing you can do to maintain motivation is:
options: Combine intrinsic enjoyment, external goals, and social connection
questions.explanation: Combining all three motivation types creates a sustainable system. No single source of motivation is enough alone.

title: My Commitment to English
description: Build commitment statements using If-Then planning.

sentences.prompt: Build a commitment using If-Then planning
sentences.correct: If I feel like skipping practice, then I will do just one flashcard.

sentences.prompt: Build a sentence about why you learn English
sentences.correct: I am learning English because I want to connect with people worldwide.
I can't speak fluently yet, but I am improving every day.
After my morning coffee, I will practice English for 10 minutes.

sentences.prompt: Build a response to the comparison trap
sentences.correct: I only compare myself with who I was three months ago.

sentences.prompt: Build a celebration of your progress
sentences.correct: I have completed 35 chapters and I am proud of my progress!

title: Growth Mindset Vocabulary
description: Transform your internal vocabulary from failure to growth.

cards.back: ✅ "I'm not good at English YET." (Growth) — "Yet" transforms a limitation into a learning process.
cards.example: I can't understand native speakers yet, but I'm improving every week.

cards.back: Perseverance and sustained passion for long-term goals. It's not talent, it's the decision not to give up.
cards.example: Learning a language requires grit — the willingness to keep going when it gets hard.

cards.back: Resist an immediate reward to obtain a greater one in the long term.
cards.example: Choosing to study instead of watching TV is an act of delayed gratification.

cards.back: Person who checks your progress regularly. It keeps you engaged.
cards.example: My accountability partner and I check in every Sunday to review our English goals.

cards.back: Belief that skills are developed with effort and practice, they are not fixed.
cards.example: With a growth mindset, every mistake is a learning opportunity.

cards.back: Ability to recover quickly from difficulties. Essential for learning.
cards.example: Resilience means getting back to studying even after a frustrating day.

cards.back: Period where there seems to be no progress. It is temporary and normal in all learning.
cards.example: I'm going through a plateau, but I know it's just part of the process.

cards.back: Connect a new habit to an existing one. "After X, I will do Y."
cards.example: After my morning coffee, I review 5 flashcards. That's habit stacking.

cards.back: Motivation that comes from within: curiosity, enjoyment, personal satisfaction.
cards.example: My intrinsic motivation for English is that I genuinely enjoy watching movies without subtitles.

cards.back: Streak or chain of consecutive days of practice. Powerful motivator.
cards.example: I have a 30-day streak! I don't want to break the chain.

cards.back: ✅ "Fluency is a journey, and I'm making progress every day." (Growth) — Reframe: proceso, no destino.
cards.example: I couldn't order food in English 6 months ago. Now I can have full conversations.

cards.back: 5-15 minute study sessions. More effective and sustainable than long irregular sessions.
cards.example: Three 10-minute micro-sessions are better than one skipped 1-hour session.

title: Barrera → Estrategia
description: Match each motivational barrier with the most effective strategy.

pairs.right: Take a level test to see real progress 📊
Set a "mistake quota" — aim for 5 errors/day 🎯
Commit to one system for 90 days 📅

pairs.left: Life gets in the way (no time)

pairs.right: Compare only with yourself 3 months ago 🪞
Start with low-stakes practice: self-talk, recording 🎙️
Switch to content you genuinely enjoy 🎬
Use spaced repetition apps like Anki 🔄

title: Vocabulario Motivacional
description: Unscramble words that help you stay motivated.

words.hint: Persistence; keep trying without giving up
Goal; goal you want to achieve
